description: 555 timer IC — monostable, astable, and bistable multivibrator. One of the most widely used ICs ever made.

pins.name: TRIG
pins.description: Trigger input. Output goes HIGH when TRIG falls below 1/3 Vcc. Active LOW.
pins.requirements: Do not leave floating. Pull high via resistor if unused. In astable mode connects to THRES.

pins.name: OUT
pins.description: Output. Swings between near-GND and near-Vcc. Can source/sink 200mA.
pins.requirements: Can drive LEDs, relays, small transistors directly. Add flyback diode if driving relay coil.

pins.name: RESET
pins.description: Active-LOW reset. Forces output LOW when pulled below 0.7V.
pins.requirements: Pull HIGH to Vcc (via 10k or direct) if reset not used. Leaving RESET floating causes erratic behavior.

pins.name: CV
pins.description: Control voltage. Normally connected to 2/3 Vcc internally. Can adjust threshold externally.
pins.requirements: If not used for control, connect 10nF–100nF cap to GND to filter noise. Do not leave floating.

pins.name: THRES
pins.description: Threshold input. Output goes LOW when THRES rises above 2/3 Vcc.
pins.requirements: In astable mode, connects to TRIG. In monostable, connects to timing capacitor positive terminal.

pins.name: DISCH
pins.description: Discharge. Open-collector transistor that discharges the timing capacitor when output is LOW.
pins.requirements: Connect to timing capacitor positive terminal via resistor Rb in astable mode. In monostable, connect to timing capacitor via Ra.

pins.name: VCC
pins.description: Positive supply voltage. 4.5V to 16V for NE555.
pins.requirements: 10nF–100nF bypass cap to GND close to pin. The 555 generates significant current spikes on the supply — always decouple.

required_passives.reason: Supply decoupling — 555 generates current spikes, must be bypassed
Noise filter on control voltage — prevents frequency jitter
Timing capacitor. f ≈ 1.44/((Ra+2Rb)×C) for astable. t ≈ 1.1×Ra×C for monostable.

application_circuits.name: Astable oscillator
application_circuits.description: Continuous square wave. f = 1.44/((Ra+2Rb)×C). Duty cycle = (Ra+Rb)/(Ra+2Rb). TRIG connects to THRES. RESET to Vcc.

application_circuits.name: Monostable (one-shot)
application_circuits.description: Pulse of width t = 1.1×R×C triggered by falling edge on TRIG. THRES and DISCH connect to C via R. RESET to Vcc.

common_mistakes: Leaving RESET (pin 4) floating causes erratic output — always pull HIGH to Vcc if not used
Leaving CV (pin 5) floating introduces noise — connect 10nF cap to GND
NE555 output current spikes couple onto Vcc — must use bypass cap on pin 8
CMOS 555 (LMC555/TLC555) should not be substituted without checking — input thresholds and output drive differ
Minimum timing resistor value ~1kΩ — lower values exceed the 200mA output current rating during discharge
notes: Most common astable formula: f = 1.44/((Ra+2Rb)×C), Duty = (Ra+Rb)/(Ra+2Rb). For 50% duty cycle use Ra≈0 (add small series R to protect discharge transistor) and equal-valued diodes to bypass Rb on charge/discharge paths. The bipolar NE555 uses ~3–6mA quiescent current; CMOS variant uses <1mA.
